definition: Knowing arising dependent on eye and visible form.
notes: The project prefers eye-knowing because it keeps the analysis close to the standard sixfold structure while aligning with the updated treatment of viññāṇa as knowing. Eye-consciousness remains an acceptable alternate where continuity with familiar doctrinal English matters.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose where the English compound needs to feel less technical
In comparative doctrinal prose using established Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when continuity with standard terminology helps.

example_phrases.pali: cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the eye and forms, eye-knowing arises
